title.en: Understanding Linking Verbs: The Meaning of 'Link'
title.zh: 你还搞不懂“系动词”？“系”到底啥意思？

summary.en: A linking verb does one simple job: it assigns the meaning after it back to the subject before it — drawing an equals sign. That linking is exactly why it's called a 'linking verb'.
summary.zh: 系动词只做一件简单的事：把它后面的含义赋予前面的主语——相当于画等号。正因为它把前后两端“连起来”，所以才叫系动词（linking verb）。

heading.en: A linking verb assigns the meaning after it to the subject
heading.zh: 系动词把后面的含义赋予前面的主语

body.en: The job of a linking verb is, plainly put, assignment: it hands the meaning that comes after it back to the part that comes before it (the subject). If you've done any programming, think of it as the assignment operator '=' — it just draws an equals sign between the two ends.
body.zh: 系动词的作用说白了就是“赋予”：把它后面的含义赋予给它前面的部分（主语）。如果你学过编程，可以把它理解成赋值运算符“=”——它只是在前后两端之间画一个等号。

concepts.sourceRef: 系动词的作用说白了就是副词，也就是把它后面的含义赋予给它之前的部分

heading.en: 'Linking' is where the name comes from

body.en: The way a linking verb assigns meaning is simply by connecting the two concepts on either side of it. Because it links the two ends together — links them — it's also called a connecting verb, a linking verb. That 'link' is the origin of the Chinese name 系动词.
body.zh: 系动词赋予含义的方式，就是简简单单地把它前后两端的概念连起来。因为它把两端“连接”起来——link 一下——所以又叫联系动词、linking verb。这个 link，就是“系动词”里那个“系”字的由来。

heading.en: Same idea even when the verb adds flavor
heading.zh: 动词带点额外含义时，原理不变

body.en: Linking verbs other than 'be' still just assign the following meaning to the subject — they only add a flavor on top. 'Smells nice' assigns 'nice' to the subject plus a layer of 'by smell'; 'remained empty' adds 'stayed unchanged'; 'grew dark' adds 'gradually increased'. Common linking verbs include keep, stay, seem, appear, feel, become, turn, prove.
body.zh: 除 be 以外的系动词，本质上也只是把后面的含义赋予主语，只不过多加一层“味道”。smells nice 是把 nice 给主语，再多一层“通过闻到”；remained empty 多一层“一直没变”；grew dark 多一层“一点点增加”。常见系动词还有 keep、stay、seem、appear、feel、become、turn、prove 等。

concepts.sourceRef: 系动词还有很多，如 keep, stay, seem, appear, feel, become, turn, prove 等等等等，但它们背后的原理都是用不同方式副词

heading.en: Don't confuse the linking-verb role with the real-verb role
heading.zh: 别把系动词身份和实义动词身份弄混

body.en: A word like 'smell' can wear two hats. As a linking verb ('The carrot smells nice') it assigns 'nice' to the carrot. But 'smell' can also be a real (notional) verb: transitive when it takes an object ('I smell something'), or intransitive when it stands alone ('it smells' = it stinks). Same spelling, different job — judge by the role in the sentence.
body.zh: 像 smell 这种词可以有两种身份。作系动词时（The carrot smells nice）它把 nice 赋予胡萝卜；但 smell 也能作实义动词：带宾语时是及物动词（I smell something），独立使用时是不及物动词（it smells，意为“它难闻”）。拼写相同、作用不同——要看它在句中的角色来判断。

concepts.sourceRef: 请一定不要把动词的系动词身份和实义动词身份弄混淆

note.en: 'Am' assigns the meaning 'a rabbit' back to 'I' — an equals sign. Chinese: 我是一只兔子.
note.zh: “am”把“a rabbit”的含义赋予“I”——画等号。中文：我是一只兔子。

note.en: 'Is' connects the two ends: it assigns 'smart' to 'the rabbit'. Chinese usually leaves 'is' untranslated: 这兔子聪明.
note.zh: “is”把前后两端连起来：把“smart”赋予“the rabbit”。中文一般不译出“is”：这兔子聪明。

note.en: The carrot isn't doing the smelling — it's being smelled. 'Smells' still assigns 'nice' to 'the carrot', just with a 'by smell' flavor. Chinese: 这胡萝卜闻起来香.
note.zh: 不是胡萝卜在闻，而是被闻。“smells”仍把“nice”赋予“the carrot”，只多一层“闻起来”的味道。中文：这胡萝卜闻起来香。

note.en: Assigns 'empty' to 'the room', plus a layer of 'stayed unchanged'. Chinese: 房间一直是空的.
note.zh: 把“empty”赋予“the room”，再加一层“一直没变”。中文：房间一直是空的。

note.en: Assigns 'dark' to 'the sky', plus a layer of 'gradually increasing'. Chinese: 天空渐渐变暗.
note.zh: 把“dark”赋予“the sky”，再加一层“一点点增加”。中文：天空渐渐变暗。

note.en: Here 'smell' is a real verb — transitive, taking the object 'something'. Not a linking verb. Chinese: 我闻到了什么.
note.zh: 这里“smell”是实义动词——及物，带宾语“something”，不是系动词。中文：我闻到了什么。

examples.text: The carrot has gone bad, it smells.

note.en: This 'smells' is a real verb too — intransitive, no object, meaning 'it stinks'. Chinese: 这胡萝卜坏了，它难闻.
note.zh: 这个“smells”也是实义动词——不及物、无宾语，意为“它难闻”。中文：这胡萝卜坏了，它难闻。

prompt.en: What does the linking verb 'is' do in 'The rabbit is smart'?
prompt.zh: 在“The rabbit is smart”中，系动词“is”起什么作用？

text.en: It assigns 'smart' to 'the rabbit', linking the two ends
text.zh: 把“smart”赋予“the rabbit”，把前后两端连起来

text.en: It performs the action of being smart

explain.en: A linking verb draws an equals sign: it hands the meaning after it ('smart') back to the subject before it ('the rabbit').
explain.zh: 系动词画等号：把它后面的含义（smart）赋予前面的主语（the rabbit）。

prompt.en: In which sentence is 'smell(s)' NOT a linking verb?
prompt.zh: 下列哪句中的“smell(s)”不是系动词？

text.zh: The carrot smells nice.（这胡萝卜闻起来香。）
I smell something.（我闻到了什么。）
The flowers smell sweet.（花闻起来很香。）

explain.en: In 'I smell something', 'smell' is a real transitive verb taking the object 'something'. In the other two it links an adjective back to the subject.
explain.zh: 在“I smell something”中，“smell”是实义及物动词，带宾语“something”；另两句则把形容词赋予主语，是系动词。

prompt.en: Because a linking verb connects its two ends, it's also called a ____ verb (English word).
prompt.zh: 因为系动词把前后两端连起来，所以它又叫 ____ 动词（英文单词）。

explain.en: It links the two ends together — hence 'linking verb', which is exactly where the Chinese name 系动词 comes from.
explain.zh: 它把两端连接起来——所以叫 linking verb，这正是“系动词”里“系”字的由来。

explain.en: A linking verb assigns its meaning to the subject, so the complement must be an adjective ('dark'), not an adverb ('darkly').
explain.zh: 系动词把含义赋予主语，所以补语（表语）要用形容词“dark”，不能用副词“darkly”。

note.en: Linking verb: 'smells' assigns 'nice' to the carrot.
Real verb, transitive — it takes the object 'something'.

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Oh no, the carrot has gone bad, it smells.

note.en: Real verb, intransitive — no object, meaning 'it stinks'.
note.zh: 实义动词，不及物——无宾语，意为“它难闻”。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: So you are a clever rabbit after all.

note.en: Back to a linking verb: 'are' draws an equals sign between 'you' and 'a clever rabbit'.
note.zh: 又回到系动词：“are”在“you”和“a clever rabbit”之间画等号。
